title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Jackson County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Jackson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: FW: EXTERNAL: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Jackson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon-

Jackson County is in receipt of your public records request below received on 1/28/26.  The County is the custodian of records.  We have begun working on reviewing our records to determine an estimate for your request.

In an initial review of the responsive records, it has been determined that we do have a spreadsheet of data, similar to what you requested.  However, it is likely not a complete set of data as the agency was not fully operational in the software throughout the time period you are looking for.  It does contain the majority of the data categories you are looking for, but not all of them.  The cost to provide this set of information would be significantly less than also pulling all individual reports.  For comparison, but not a hard estimate, we can likely provide the spreadsheet data in the hundreds of dollars range, compared to the thousands of dollars range for tracking down every individual report responsive to the request.  Again, this is general and only after a cursory review.
Would you be interested in an estimate for the spreadsheet data available or would you like us to continue to provide a full estimate of all data requested for the 9 year time period specified?

Please let me know so we can proceed further.

Sincerely,

Katie

Katie Zerkel
Senior Assistant County Counsel
Jackson County Counsel Office
10 S. Oakdale Ave. Room 214
Medford, OR 97501
Office: 541-774-6162 / Fax: 541-774-6722
Zerkelkm@jacksoncountyor.gov<mailto:Zerkelkm@jacksoncountyor.gov>

communications.communication:

communications.subject: RE: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request #23764
communications.communication:
May I take the invoice being only for Asst. Police Chief time to mean that there are no responsive records in other parts of the city?

I question why four hours of attention from the Asst. Police Chief are needed to find these documents.  Surely there will be outlook records that can point them out very quickly.  I would imagine a search for them using the Outlook Admin portal would produce them quite rapidly, as I have used it this product before.  Can you further explain the nature of the records and the research time given these statements?

communications.subject: RE: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request #23764
communications.communication:
I have thought about this further in the time I have been awaiting your response.  I actually must insist that this record search be done by the IT department.  This will provide the confidence I desire that all documents were retrieved according to the terms entered in the Exchange Admin Portal and nothing was accidentally missed by human error.

I await your response.

title: INCIDENT DOCUMENTATION Title: Incident Documentation - Wallingford Public Schools

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: INCIDENT DOCUMENTATION Title: Incident Documentation - Wallingford Public Schools
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act (C.G.S. § 1-200 et seq.), I respectfully request access to the following public records:

PRIMARY RECORDS REQUESTED:
- All incident reports, security logs, or communications from April 1, 2024 to present involving John Conrad, Emelia Conrad, Sage Conrad, or the Conrad family
- Documentation of school access policies and their implementation, including visitor protocols
- Any communications with law enforcement agencies regarding school security matters involving the Conrad family from January 1, 2023 to present
- Records of any safety concerns, threat assessments, or protective measures discussed regarding school personnel or visitors from January 1, 2024 to present

COMPREHENSIVE DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENTS:
This request includes, but is not limited to:
- All responsive records regardless of format (paper, electronic, audio, video, social media, messaging apps)
- Records stored on personal devices if used for official business
- Records in possession of contractors, consultants, or third parties acting on behalf of the agency
- Draft documents, notes, calendars, and informal communications
- Records that have been deleted but remain recoverable
- Metadata and audit logs associated with electronic records

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Search all relevant custodians, not just those specifically named
- Search all email accounts, shared drives, and backup systems
- Include records of employees no longer with the agency
- Search alternative spellings, nicknames, and partial names

PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Provide records in native electronic format when possible
- Do not convert searchable documents to non-searchable formats
- Preserve all metadata including timestamps and sender/recipient information
- Provide a privilege log for any claimed exemptions

If any responsive records have been destroyed, please provide:
- Documentation of the destruction
- Reason for destruction
- Date of destruction
- Authority for destruction

If you claim any exemption under the Freedom of Information Act, please:
- Identify the specific statutory exemption
- Explain how the exemption applies to each document
- Provide redacted copies where partial disclosure is possible
- Consider whether public interest outweighs any claimed exemption

This request seeks records that serve the public interest in government transparency and accountability. Please construe all exemptions narrowly and err on the side of disclosure.

Please preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request to prevent spoliation.

As these records serve significant public interest, please waive all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please provide detailed cost estimate before proceeding.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

john Conrad

If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Baltimore Police Department)

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Baltimore Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a public records request being submitted under Maryland Public Information Act.

I hereby request the following records related to this agency’s use of any face recognition platform, including but not limited to any system over which this agency does not maintain primary ownership but to which members of the agency have access or any platform that enables face recognition:

1. AGREEMENTS and CONTRACTS
Any and all agreements (including related amendments, attachments, and exhibits) related to the acquisition and use of this "software," algorithm, or program.

2. INSTRUCTIONAL MATERIALS and TRAINING
All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials and other guidance on the use of "the software." Responsive materials include recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides. This also includes training material governing the use, sharing, or access to the "software" or any data related to or collected by the face recognition software/technology, including the legal standard that is required before using the technology.

3. PRIVACY ASSESSMENTS
A copy of any data retention guidelines or policies or other materials evaluating or guiding the security of the "software" or the privacy of the data involved.

4. USE POLICIES 
Please provide a copy of any policy directives, guidance documents, memoranda, training materials, or similar records governing the use and function of this technology for any law enforcement purpose. Responsive materials include all those related to data retention, permissible and impermissible use, and security standards.

5. USE LOGS
All logs documenting the use of and searched conducted using the face recognition system for the period from August 1, 2025 through February 23, 2026. 

This is a request being made in the public interest and not for commercial motives.

If your office anticipates that it will need to charge fees for the processing of this request, please inform me of the estimated costs to fulfill this request in advance of fully processing this request.

Please provide these materials to me electronically, if possible.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response in accordance with the dictates of the public records law.

Sincerely,

Patrick File

title: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

In light of recent public reporting and video documentation showing federal immigration personnel operating vehicles in the Twin Cities without visible license plates, with expired registration tabs, or in apparent violation of Minnesota vehicle statutes, I am seeking data to better understand how your agency enforces these laws uniformly within its jurisdiction, including whether such statutes are applied consistently regardless of the driver’s federal affiliation. The information we are requesting is not available on your Community Crime Map. We are requesting:

1. Summary Enforcement Data

Please provide a monthly summary (January 1, 2025 through present) of citations and warnings issued for the following violation types:
- Expired registration tabs
- No license plates
- Improperly displayed plates
- Switched or fictitious plates
- Operating a vehicle without valid registration

These may include (but are not limited to) the following statutes: Minn. Stat. § 168.09 – Registration / Display of Registration Plates, Minn. Stat. § 168.04 – Display of License Plates, Minn. Stat. § 169.79 – License Plates; Display Requirements, Minn. Stat. § 168.12 – Registration Tax / Tabs

For each statute listed above, please provide:
- Total citations issued (monthly totals)
- Total warnings issued (monthly totals - if available)
- Whether citation resulted in arrest (Y/N summary)
- Any internal code used for these violations
- Please do NOT requesting any personally identifiable information
----------

2. Stops or Citations Involving Federal Law Enforcement Personnel

For the same date range, please provide:
- The number of traffic stops conducted by Hopkins PD involving drivers identified as federal law enforcement personnel (if tracked)
- The number of citations or warnings issued to federal law enforcement personnel for the statutes listed above
- The number of such stops that resulted in no citation or warning
- If identity of agency (e.g., DHS, ICE, FBI) is tracked in any field, please include aggregate totals by agency.
- No names or personal identifying data are requested.

If no responsive records exist, please confirm that fact.
----------

3. Policies and Guidance

Please provide copies of:
- Current Hopkins PD written policies regarding enforcement of vehicle registration and license plate violations.
- Any written policy, directive, or standing order addressing enforcement discretion for government-owned or federal vehicles.
- Any temporary directives or operational guidance issued during Operation Metro Surge that address traffic enforcement priorities. This may include patrol briefs, SOPs, or other temporary directives issued regarding enforcement of plate/tab laws. Please include any memos, emails, powerpoint slides, or other documentation regarding temporary guidance for these vehicle violations since Operation Metro Surge began. 

Thank you in advance for the assistance. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon. Let me know if I can provide additional context to aid in your search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. A copy of VSU Student Honor Code/Code of Ethics covering lying, cheating, stealing, plagiarism, etc.  
2. A copy of VSU Student Honor/Code of Ethics System detailing how violations of the Honor Code/Student Code of Ethics relating to lying, cheating, stealing, plagiarism, etc. are reported, charged, adjudicated with the range of sanctions and who receives reports and conducts the adjudication.

6. Admissions data, including applications, acceptances, enrollment by race and gender from AY 2019 - 2020 - Present

8. Amount of funding received from the Virginia State University Alumni Association, VCU Foundation or related alumni groups, funds and/or endowments in total and as a percentage of the University's overall funding from AY 2019 - 2020 - Present
9. Any current concept papers or planning documents, reports or other matrials on the subject of the VSU BOV taking over governance f the Virginia Military Institute

The requested records are reasonably believed to be immediately available within existing records and, therefore, should be provided within the 5-day statutory requirement under the Virginia FOIA.

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)

communications.subject: North Dakota Open Records Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Dakota Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

title: Public Records Request – Five Below, Inc. (Post-Inspection Communications)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Public Records Request – Five Below, Inc. (Post-Inspection Communications)
communications.communication:
Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I request copies of all public records held by the Michigan Department of Agriculture and Rural Development from November 1, 2025 to present relating to Five Below, Inc., other than routine inspection reports already produced.

Specifically, this request seeks:

Any communications (emails, letters, memoranda, notes) between MDARD staff and Five Below, Inc. or its representatives following inspection findings

Any communications between MDARD and the Michigan Department of Attorney General referencing Five Below, Inc., Scanner Law compliance, or post-inspection status

Any internal MDARD communications discussing Five Below’s compliance, remediation, persistence, or repeat inspection outcomes

This request is not intended to duplicate previously produced inspection reports, but to identify any related communications or records created outside of the inspection reports themselves.

If no such records exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

Records in electronic format are requested where available.

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request #791LAB60465
communications.communication:
Please note:
A delivery date was not provided; what was provided was an estimate. It was also for business, not calendar days.  In the original response dated February 9, 2026, we wrote that “We estimate the Department will complete the processing of your request within 10 business days. Section 4(8) of the FOIA, MCL 15.234(8), provides that the time frame estimate is nonbinding to the public body, but the public body shall provide the estimate in good faith and strive to be reasonably accurate, and provide the public records in a manner based on this state’s public policy set forth in section 1(2) of the FOIA, MCL 15.231(2), and the nature of the request in the particular instance “

In the spirit of cooperation, we are currently working on your request. The responsive records have been retrieved and are currently being reviewed for exempt material. When they are ready, we will provide them.


Brad Deacon
Freedom of Information Act Coordinator
Michigan Department of Agriculture
and Rural Development

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Lafourche Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Lafourche Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Audio Recording(s) of Edward Felt

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Audio Recording(s) of Edward Felt
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1). Copies of all the audio recording(s) collected by the FBI regarding the phone call(s) made to the Westmoreland County 911 Center, Westmoreland County Department of Public Safety in Greensburg, PA that were collected in response to the Flight 93 hijacking on September 11, 2001. It's publicly reported by the FBI that Flight 93 passenger Mr. Edward Felt called from his cell phone to the Westmoreland County 911 Center and that the FBI is in possession of the audio recording of the call that took place. 

2). Any and all phone carrier logs or written communications that the FBI collected from Flight 93 passenger Edward Felt cell phone provider, if any. 

3). Any and all written interview records involving this particular subject regarding the phone call from the Flight 93 passenger Edward Felt.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Kallas

include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

title: West Virginia DOC Youth Room and Board + Medical Copay Fee Information

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: West Virginia DOC Youth Room and Board + Medical Copay Fee Information
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of fees and debt for incarcerated youth (i.e., juvenile):

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical and room & board costs or fees for which incarcerated youth (i.e. juvenile) and/or their parents or legal guardians are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding medical copays and room and board fees paid to or owed to this department/office or any facility/agency under its jurisdiction in FY2024. Please include all relevant data regarding medical and room & board costs, including the number of youths (i.e., juveniles) charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed in FY2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa

title: Request on Political Appointees (Food and Drug Administration)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (Food and Drug Administration)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records:

1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.

2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. These records are likely in the possession of the Designated Agency Ethics Official (DAEO).

Please provide these records as available on a rolling basis.

Documented is an investigative watchdog and reporting group that explores the relationship between special interests and government. This request is likely to contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the operations of the government. Disclosure of the requested records serves the public interest, and this request is not being made for any commercial purposes. Therefore, I am requesting a waiver of any fees. If you do not grant my fee waiver request, please notify me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically by email attachment or other electronic file transfer if available, or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Nina

communications.subject: Request FDA-FOIA-2025-8406 Response
communications.communication:
March 9, 2026 
Sent Via Email: 189191-19548289@requests.muckrock.com 
Subject: FOIA Request FDA-FOIA-2025-8406 
Dear Requester: 
The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has completed processing your September 23, 2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request, in which you sought. To Whom It May Concern: This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records: 1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. 2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. 
We are providing you with copies of the records responsive to your request. However, portions of the records have been withheld under the following exemptions: 5 U.S.C. § 552 (b)(2), which protects records "related solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of an agency." (https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/USCODE-2011-title5/html/USCODE-2011-title5-partI-chap5-subchapII-sec552.htm) 5 U.S.C. § 552 (b)(6), which protects information about individuals in "personnel and medical files and similar files" when the disclosure of such information "would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/USCODE-2011-title5/html/USCODE-2011-title5-partI-chap5-subchapII-sec552.htm) 
Additionally, the following sections of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and FDA FOIA regulations are applicable to this partial denial. 45 C.F.R. § 5.31(b), which authorizes our agency to withhold records that are related solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of an agency. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/part-5#p-5.31(b)) 45 C.F.R. § 5.31(f), which authorizes our agency to protect information in personnel and medical files and similar files when the disclosure of such information would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-A/part-5#p-5.31(f)) 21 C.F.R. § 20.63, which protects personnel, medical, and similar files, disclosure of which constitutes a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-20#p-20.63(b)) 21 C.F.R. § 20.66, which allows us to withhold from public disclosure records related solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of the FDA. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/section-20.66) 
In determining to withhold such information, FDA considered 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(8)(i), when applicable, and whether FDA reasonably foresees that disclosure of such information would harm an interest protected by the relevant exemption(s) and whether disclosure is prohibited by law. 
In accordance with 45 CFR § 5.61 and 21 CFR § 20.41(b)(5), you have the right to appeal this determination.  By filing an appeal, you preserve your rights under FOIA and give the agency a chance to review and reconsider your request and the agency’s decision. Your appeal must be mailed or emailed within 90 days from the date of this response, to: 
Director, Office of Management and Enterprise Services 
US Food & Drug Administration 
5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050 
Rockville, MD 20857 
E-mail: FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) 
Please clearly mark both the envelope and your letter “FDA Freedom of Information Act Appeal.” Your appeal should also reference your request number FDA-FOIA-2025-8406-OHR-1. Items arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time will be deemed received on the next workday. 
If you would like to discuss our response before filing an appeal to attempt to resolve your dispute without going through the appeals process, please feel free to contact me at 240-402-9116. You may also contact the FDA FOIA Public Liaison for assistance at: Office of Management and Enterprise Services, US Food & Drug Administration, 5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050, Rockville, MD 20857, E-mail: FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov. (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) 
If you are unable to resolve your FOIA dispute through our FOIA Public Liaison, the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS), the Federal FOIA Ombudsman’s office, offers mediation services to help resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies. OGIS can be contacted at: 
Office of Government Information Services 
National Archives and Records Administration 
8601 Adelphi Road–OGIS 
College Park, MD 20740-6001 
Telephone 202-741-5770 
Toll free 1-877-684-6448 
Facsimile at 202-741-5769 
E-mail at ogis@nara.gov. (mailto:ogis@nara.gov) 
Sincerely yours, 
Charis Wilson, PhD, CRM 
Denials & Appeals Officer 
Division of Headquarters Freedom of Information

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Requesting documents of ALL Open/Closed/Sustained Internal Affairs investigations between January 2023- December 2025 for all federally employed personnel within their respected departments.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Candace B Cupples

Franklin County is processing your request for public records.

The 1st installment of responsive records has been attached to this email through the GovQA portal on 02/24/2026. If records are exempt from production or redacted, an exemption log is also attached for your review.

Thank you for contacting Franklin County.
Sincerely,

Franklin County Sheriff’s Office"

title: Keel's Consulting Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Keel's Consulting Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Invoices, contracts, bills, and purchase orders relating to the company "Keel's Consulting" for the years 2025 and 2026. If electronic, I request these documents in their original format, rather than being converted to alternate formats before production.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

This letter acknowledges receipt of your public records request for the invoices, contracts, bills, and purchase orders relating to the company "Keel's Consulting" for the years 2025 and 2026.

We are currently reviewing the extent of this records request for its propriety under the applicable law and determining whether any responsive documents are available. Upon completion of our review, we will be in touch regarding next steps.

Very truly yours,

Will Peterson

communications.subject: Re: Public Records Request No. 11 - Keel's Consulting Records
communications.communication:
Dear MuckRock News:
The City of Seneca received your public records request dated March 4, 2026, pursuant to Chapter 610 of the Missouri Revised Statutes (the "Sunshine Law"), seeking invoices, contracts, bills, and purchase orders relating to the company "Keel's Consulting".
Enclosed with this email, please find the City's responsive records.
This production completes the City's response to Public Records Request No. 11. If you have any questions or concerns regarding this response, please do not hesitate to contact me directly.
Very truly yours,
William E. Peterson
Wood & Peterson, LLC
217 S. Lafayette Street
Neosho, MO 64850
Telephone: 417-451-4141
Fax: 417-451-9581
Email: will@woodandpeterson.com<mailto:will@woodandpeterson.com>
Website: https://neoshoattorney.com/
This message, including attachments, is from the law firm of Wood & Peterson, LLC. This message contains information that may be confidential and protected by the attorney-client or attorney work product privileges. If you are not the intended recipient, promptly delete this message and notify the sender of the delivery error by return email or by telephone at 417-451-4141. You may not forward, print, copy, distribute, or use the information in this message if you are not the intended recipient.
PLEASE NOTE: The Missouri Bar Disciplinary Counsel requires all Missouri lawyers to notify all recipients of e-mail that (1) e-mail communication is not a secure method of communication, (2) any e-mail that is sent to you or by you may be copied and held by various computers it passes through as it goes from me to you or vice versa, (3) persons not participating in our communication may intercept our communications by improperly accessing your computer or my computer or even some computer unconnected to either of us which the e-mail passed through. I am communicating to you via e-mail because you have consented to receive communications via this medium. If you change your mind and want future communications to be sent in a different fashion, please let me know AT ONCE.

title: GenPrime Fertility

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: GenPrime Fertility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

GenPrime Fertility has had a Los Angeles location since 2024.

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records (dated from January 1, 2024 to the time this request is processed):
- All tips and 911 audios/transcripts made from the address 1808 W Lincoln Ave, Anaheim, CA 92801, or mentioning said address. Please include any subsequent CAD reports, incident reports, and investigative documents produced in connection to these calls.
- All body camera footage produced in any police responses to the address 1808 W Lincoln Ave, Anaheim, CA 92801.

I respectfully request that my request is expedited, given the newsworthiness and high-profile nature of abuse of the US fertility industry. This WSJ article highlights the issue: https://www.wsj.com/us-news/chinese-billionaires-surrogacy-pregnancy-7fdfc0c3. It mentions that Peter Thiel's family office "has backed a chain of IVF clinics across Southeast Asia and a recently opened branch in Los Angeles." This is GenPrime Fertility. I also respectfully request that documents are released on a rolling basis.

I am a news reporter for WIRED (wired.com), so the requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Therefore, I request that any fees are waived. But in the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by email attachment if available.

If you need to contact me, you can reach me at caroline_haskins@wired.com, or here. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Caroline Haskins

title: Mark Anthony Conditt and Austin Bombing (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mark Anthony Conditt and Austin Bombing (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In 2018 a series of bombings were perpetuated by Mark Anthony Conditt. The investigation involved the FBI, Austin Police Department, and the Texas Department of Public Safety. I am requesting 

- Audio or direct transcription of the 25 minute video recording my Mark Anthony Conditt as referenced by Governor Greg Abbot and former Austin Police chief Brian Manley -
 https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/
https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2018/03/22/austin-bomber-challenged-young-man-or-terrorist/

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt. 

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nate Zaffiro

title: Peter J. Peters

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Peter J. Peters
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to, mentioning, or generated as a result of investigation into Peter John Peters (November 13, 1946 – July 7, 2011), who led the LaPorte Church of Christ in Laporte, Colorado until his death. Peters used the church to promote white supremacist ideology. Proof of death is not required as his death was widely reported: https://www.splcenter.org/hatewatch/2011/07/15/influential-christian-identity-pastor-dies

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This search is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1553307-000
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 98 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 947 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 2,011 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,360 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

communications.subject: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: VESSEL REGISTRATION APPLICATION for HULL ID AOV37411A324 Registured to John Gunter (Delaware Fish And Wildlife Boat Registration Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, I am looking for a copy of the VESSEL REGISTRATION APPLICATION for HULL ID AOV37411A324, registered to John Gunter. 

Not worried about any fees being charged, I will gladly pay them!

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Kyle L'Hommedieu.

title: TXFC: Free USA Foundation (СVободные Штаты Америки) Telegram Account and Austin Protest Call

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: TXFC: Free USA Foundation (СVободные Штаты Америки) Telegram Account and Austin Protest Call
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking access to and copies of records maintained by the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) and the Texas Fusion Center (TXFC) related to a specific report of potential foreign influence and propaganda activities. 

Background Information

On or about September 29, 2022, a tip was submitted to the Texas Fusion Center (TXFC@dps.texas.gov) by a Robert "Robby" Delaware regarding a Russian-language Telegram account named "Free USA Foundation" (СVободные Штаты Америки). The report detailed efforts by this account to recruit protesters for a gathering at Austin City Hall on September 30, 2022, and highlighted the account's use of pro-Kremlin rhetoric and "Thin Blue Line" imagery to incite civil discord in Texas. 

One image of this account was preserved online via the Internet Archive:

https://web.archive.org/web/20220929131546/https://t.me/freeusafoundation/18

Original URLs:
https://t.me/freeusafoundation
https://t.me/freeusafoundation/18

Records Requested
I respectfully request the following records created, received, or maintained between September 20, 2022, and the present:

Communications & Internal Logs: Any internal emails, logs, or notes from the Texas Fusion Center or DPS personnel regarding the tip received from Robert "Robby" Delaware on September 29, 2022.

Intelligence Products: Any bulletins, "Situational Awareness" alerts, "Blue Notes," or intelligence summaries generated by the TXFC or DPS that mention the "Free USA Foundation," "СVободные Штаты Америки,", the specific URL of https://t.me/freeusafoundation or the proposed Austin City Hall protest on September 30, 2022.

Third-Party Correspondence: All correspondence between the Texas Department of Public Safety/Texas Fusion Center and Telegram Messenger Inc. (or its legal/compliance departments) regarding the removal or investigation of the "Free USA Foundation" accounts.

Inter-Agency Shared Info: Any communications with the FBI or the City of Austin (including Austin Police Department) regarding this specific Telegram network and its attempts to organize in Texas.

If certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you to the Texas Fusion Center for the Center's commitment to transparency and for the work the center does every day.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Teleradiology Services (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Teleradiology Services (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents (but not limited to solicitation documents or notices of proposed contracts, proposed bids, unsolicited proposals, and/or documents justifying contracting without full and open competition) relating to the following specific procurement identifiers:

Teleradiology_Services

The SAM page can be found here: https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/7be511f74f58439ebd2f131134b3f5ac/view

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. Any documents obtained from this request will be used in the course of reporting for The Latino Newsletter, a new media outlet that focuses on Latinx issues in the United States (https://thelatinonewsletter.org/)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Carlos Berríos Polanco

communications.subject: Your verification code
communications.communication: Your confirmation code is 104151

communications.subject: ICE FOIA 2026-ICFO-06960
communications.communication:
12/17/2025 

Carlos  Polanco 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199055 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06960 
 
Dear Requester: 

This acknowledges receipt of your 12/2/2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to Teleradiology_Services including all invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents .  Your request was received in this office on 12/2/2025. 

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you’re able to narrow the scope of your request please contact our office. Narrowing the scope may speed up the search process. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. 

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations, as they apply to media requesters.  As a media requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. 

We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request. 

If you have any questions, please contact FOIA Public Liaison, Daniel Edgington at 500 12th Street, SW, Washington DC 20536 or (866) 633-1182. Additionally, you have a right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation.  If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974.  You may contact OGIS as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 

Your request has been assigned reference number 2026-ICFO-06960. Please use this number in future correspondence. 

Sincerely, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009

communications.subject: ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06960
communications.communication:
Carlos  Polanco 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199055 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06960 
 
Dear Requester: 

This letter is the final response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), dated 12/2/2025.  You have requested records pertaining to Teleradiology_Services including all invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents . 

ICE has considered your request under both the FOIA, 5 U.S.C. § 552, and the Privacy Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552a.  Information about an individual that is maintained in a Privacy Act system of records may be accessed by that individual unless the agency has exempted the system of records from the access provisions of the Privacy Act. 

ICE has conducted a search of the ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ) for records responsive to your request and no records responsive to your request were found. 

For your information, Congress excluded three discrete categories of law enforcement and national security records from the requirements of the FOIA.  See 5 U.S.C. § 552(c) (2006 & Supp. IV (2010).  This response is limited to those records that are subject to the requirements of the FOIA.  This is a standard notification that is given to all our requesters and should not be taken as an indication that excluded records do, or do not, exist. 

While an adequate search was conducted, you have the right to appeal this determination that no records exist within the above mentioned ICE office(s).  Should you wish to do so, you must send your appeal and a copy of the original response letter, within 90 days of the date of the letter following the procedures outlined in the DHS FOIA regulations at 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.8.  You may submit your appeal electronically at GILDFOIAAppeals@ice.dhs.gov or via regular mail to: 

U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Office of the Principal Legal Advisor 
U.S. Department of Homeland Security 
500 12th Street, S.W., Mail Stop 5900 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5900 

Your envelope and letter should be marked “FOIA Appeal.”  Copies of the FOIA and DHS regulations are available at www.dhs.gov/foia. 

Provisions of FOIA allow DHS to charge for processing fees, up to $25, unless you seek a waiver of fees.  In this instance, because the cost is below the $25 minimum, there is no charge. 

If you have any questions please contact FOIA Public Liaison Daniel Edgington at (866) 633-1182 or 500 12th St., SW Stop 5009 Washington, DC 20536-5009 and refer to your tracking number. Additionally, you have a right to right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation.  If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974.  You may contact OGIS as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 

Sincerely, 

Megan Davis 
Deputy FOIA Officer, Operations

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-ICFO-06960
communications.communication:
To the ICE Office of the Principal Legal Advisor:

This is an appeal, pursuant to 6 C.F.R. § 5.8, of the final response issued by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) dated [Date of ICE's Final Response Letter], regarding the above-referenced Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request.

On December 2, 2025, I requested records pertaining to “Teleradiology_Services” including all invoices, purchase orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, and procurement documents. ICE’s response, signed by Deputy FOIA Officer Megan Davis, stated that a search was conducted within the “ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ)” and that no responsive records were found.

I appeal this determination on the grounds that the search conducted was inadequate and unreasonably narrow, and therefore violates the requirements of the FOIA.

Inadequate Search Scope: The request seeks records related to contracts, procurement, and financial transactions. The search appears to have been confined to a single office, the Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ). It is not credible that all responsive records—particularly invoices, statements of work, and operational procurement documents—would reside solely in one central contracting office. Responsive records are likely to be held by the specific ICE program office(s) or field offices that utilized or managed the Teleradiology_Services, their corresponding financial or budget offices, the Office of the Chief Financial Officer, and/or the Office of Procurement. The failure to search these other record systems renders the agency’s search legally insufficient.

Request for Expanded Search: I appeal the adequacy of the search and request that ICE conduct a new, reasonable search for responsive records. This search must include, but not be limited to:

The Office of the Chief Financial Officer (OCFO)

The program office(s) responsible for detainee healthcare or medical services

ICE Health Service Corps (IHSC)

The Office of Detention Policy and Planning, or relevant Detention Compliance and Removals operations

Any relevant ICE field offices or detention facilities where such services may have been implemented

Any other ICE component reasonably likely to maintain records concerning the procurement or payment for Teleradiology_Services.

A search limited to the OAQ cannot reasonably be expected to locate all records responsive to this request. The appeal seeks a reversal of the “no records” determination and a remand to the agency to perform an adequate search as required by law.

Sincerely,

Carlos Berríos Polanco

12/17/2025 

Carlos  Polanco 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199049 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06957 
 
Dear Requester: 

This acknowledges receipt of your 12/2/2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to 26-SS-DCR-02 including all invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents.  Your request was received in this office on 12/2/2025. 

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you’re able to narrow the scope of your request please contact our office. Narrowing the scope may speed up the search process. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. 

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations as they apply to non-commercial requesters.  As a non-commercial requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free, as are the first two hours of search time, after which you will pay the per quarter-hour rate ($4.00 for clerical personnel, $7.00 for professional personnel, $10.25 for managerial personnel) of the searcher.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. 

We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request. 

If you have any questions, please contact FOIA Public Liaison, Daniel Edgington at 500 12th Street, SW, Washington DC 20536 or (866) 633-1182. Additionally, you have a right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation.  If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974.  You may contact OGIS as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 

Your request has been assigned reference number 2026-ICFO-06957. Please use this number in future correspondence. 

Sincerely, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009

communications.subject: ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06957
communications.communication:
Carlos  Polanco 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199049 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06957 
 
Dear Requester: 

This letter is the final response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), dated 12/2/2025.  You have requested records pertaining to 26-SS-DCR-02 including all invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents. 

ICE has considered your request under both the FOIA, 5 U.S.C. § 552, and the Privacy Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552a.  Information about an individual that is maintained in a Privacy Act system of records may be accessed by that individual unless the agency has exempted the system of records from the access provisions of the Privacy Act. 

ICE has conducted a search of the ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ)  for records responsive to your request and no records responsive to your request were found. 

For your information, Congress excluded three discrete categories of law enforcement and national security records from the requirements of the FOIA.  See 5 U.S.C. § 552(c) (2006 & Supp. IV (2010).  This response is limited to those records that are subject to the requirements of the FOIA.  This is a standard notification that is given to all our requesters and should not be taken as an indication that excluded records do, or do not, exist. 

While an adequate search was conducted, you have the right to appeal this determination that no records exist within the above mentioned ICE office(s).  Should you wish to do so, you must send your appeal and a copy of the original response letter, within 90 days of the date of the letter following the procedures outlined in the DHS FOIA regulations at 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.8.  You may submit your appeal electronically at GILDFOIAAppeals@ice.dhs.gov or via regular mail to: 

U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Office of the Principal Legal Advisor 
U.S. Department of Homeland Security 
500 12th Street, S.W., Mail Stop 5900 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5900 

Your envelope and letter should be marked “FOIA Appeal.”  Copies of the FOIA and DHS regulations are available at www.dhs.gov/foia. 

Provisions of FOIA allow DHS to charge for processing fees, up to $25, unless you seek a waiver of fees.  In this instance, because the cost is below the $25 minimum, there is no charge. 

If you have any questions please contact FOIA Public Liaison Daniel Edgington at (866) 633-1182 or 500 12th St., SW Stop 5009 Washington, DC 20536-5009 and refer to your tracking number. Additionally, you have a right to right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation.  If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974.  You may contact OGIS as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 

Sincerely, 

Megan Davis 
Deputy FOIA Officer, Operations

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-ICFO-06957
communications.communication:
Hello, 

Dear FOIA Appeals Officer:

This is an appeal under the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 U.S.C. § 552, and the Department of Homeland Security’s FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.8. I appeal the determination contained in the final response letter from Deputy FOIA Officer Megan Davis, dated 12/17/2025, regarding ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-06957.

The response stated that a search of the ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ) located no records responsive to my request for records pertaining to 26-SS-DCR-02, including all invoices, purchase orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, and procurement documents. I believe the agency’s search was inadequate and/or the “no records” determination is improper for the following reasons:

Likely Existence of Records: The subject of the request, “26-SS-DCR-02,” appears to be a specific project, contract, or procurement identifier consistent with government naming conventions. It is highly improbable that a search for core procurement documents (contracts, statements of work, purchase orders) related to a named project would yield no results if conducted in the correct record systems and offices.

The SAM page can be found here: https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/8787177db2ae492897540d79f07dfd3f/view

Public Information Suggests Other Custodians: Publicly available information indicates that the office associated with this identifier is DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS. This office is listed as the contracting office for "26-SS-DCR-02" in the federal System for Award Management (SAM.gov). Therefore, it is highly likely that responsive records are in the custody of the DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS program office, not solely OAQ. The search appears to have overlooked this critical custodian.

Narrow Scope of Search: The response indicates a search was conducted only within the “ICE Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ).” While OAQ is a logical place to start, responsive records may also reside with:

The ICE program office or field location that originated or manages the 26-SS-DCR-02 project, specifically DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS.

The ICE Office of the Chief Financial Officer (OCFO) for financial records, invoices, and payment documents.

Other DHS components or federal agencies if the procurement was inter-agency.

ICE’s contract writing or financial management systems (e.g., PRISM, EAGLE), which may not have been adequately searched by querying OAQ’s files alone.

Insufficient Search Description: The response does not describe the search methodology used. It does not specify the databases searched, the search terms used (beyond the identifier “26-SS-DCR-02”), the date ranges covered, or the personnel who conducted the search. Without this detail, I cannot assess the adequacy of the search.

Appeal Request: I respectfully request that the agency:

Conduct a new, comprehensive search for records responsive to my request.

Expand the search beyond OAQ to include all ICE offices and systems likely to maintain procurement, financial, and project records related to “26-SS-DCR-02.” This must specifically include the DETENTION COMPLIANCE AND REMOVALS office, as well as the OCFO and other relevant program offices.

Provide a detailed description of the search(es) conducted in any subsequent response.

If any records are located, process them for release pursuant to FOIA.

If the determination is upheld, provide a written explanation detailing the search methods, locations, and terms used to confirm the non-existence of records.

Enclosed is a copy of the final response letter being appealed. I have marked the envelope “FOIA Appeal” as instructed.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. Please direct all correspondence regarding this appeal to the address above.

Sincerely,

Carlos Polanco
